modules.name: eth_pretty_events.alchemy_utils
documentation: Module with util functions to deal with Alchemy Webhooks

functions.name: eth_pretty_events.alchemy_utils.graphql_log_to_log_receipt
documentation: Convert the data in the format of Alchemy webhooks into LogReceipt

methods.name: eth_pretty_events.block_tree.BlockTree.add_block
documentation:
Adds a new block to the BlockTree.

The resulting .forks structure has the following invariants:
1. len(f) > 0 for f in self.forks
2. f[i+1].number = f[i].number + 1
3. f[i+1].hash is a block with parent_hash = f[i].hash

Returns the number of fork where the block was added

methods.name: eth_pretty_events.block_tree.BlockTree.clean
documentation:
Cleans blocks older than X blocks from the last one

Used to avoid increasing the memory usage forever

methods.name: eth_pretty_events.address_book.AddressBook.addr_to_name
documentation: Returns the name associated with a given address, if found, otherwise returns the same addr

methods.name: eth_pretty_events.address_book.AddressBook.name_to_addr
documentation: Returns the address associated with a given name, if found, otherwise returns None

methods.name: eth_pretty_events.address_book.AddressBook.has_addr

classes.name: eth_pretty_events.types.NamedTupleDictMixin
documentation: Class to adapt a named tuple to behave as a dict

methods.name: eth_pretty_events.event_parser.EventDefinition._merge_events
documentation:
The 'indexed' isn't part of the topic, so we might have different ABIs that are applicable to the
same event.

For example ERC-20 vs ERC-721 Transfer signatures are:
event Transfer(address indexed _from, address indexed _to, uint256 _value)
event Transfer(address indexed _from, address indexed _to, uint256 indexed _tokenId);

So, in some cases one signature will work and the other won't. We store both abis and when parsing
it tries all.

functions.name: eth_pretty_events.cli.load_events
documentation:
Loads all the events found in .json in the provided paths

Args:
  paths (list<str>): list of paths to walk to read the ABIs

Returns:
  int: Number of events found
